title: Jays Drop Third Straight, Lose Series vs. White Sox
summary: Sho Alli and Jesse Rubinoff break down the Blue Jays' 6-3 loss vs. the White Sox to cap a second straight series loss. They take your calls and texts, and get right to Tyler Heineman's mental errors - what will the playing time for him look like given the call-up of Brandon Valenzuela? They also discuss Brendon Little's meltdown in the sixth - with an option remaining, is it finally time for him to try and solve the issues in the minors? Why was he brought in on back-to-back days? Plus, they discuss the usage of Addison Barger and Nathan Lukes, Vladimir Guerrero Jr. hitting his first dinger of the year, and Lazaro Estrada providing the bullpen with four innings of relief work.   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Jays Lose Kirk to Injury, Get Walked Off by White Sox in Extras
summary: Jeff Blair and Kevin Barker break down the Blue Jays' 5-4 extra innings loss vs. the White Sox to start the three-game series in Chicago. They take your calls and texts, and get right to the thumb injury that drove Alejandro Kirk out of the game in extra innings - after Tyler Heineman, what does the catching depth look like? Should Heineman have held onto that ball on the bunt single in extras? They also discuss Dylan Cease allowing two earned runs over 4.1 IP, Jeff Hoffman being on the hook at the end of the game, and Vladimir Guerrero Jr.'s quiet start to the season.   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Gausman Spins 10 K Gem, Jays Drop Series vs. Rockies
summary: Jeff Blair and Kevin Barker break down the Blue Jays' 2-1 extra innings loss vs. the Rockies to drop the series in Toronto. They take your calls and texts, and discuss the offence going ice cold - through six games, can any conclusions be drawn? Does the team need more from Vladimir Guerrero Jr. and the team's stars? They get into Brendon Little, who did allow the Rockies' game-winning run - despite the result, did the process look better? Plus, Kevin Gausman carves up the Rockies for another 10 Ks - what could his season look like if this keeps up?   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Field Access: Andrés Giménez, Braydon Fisher, David Popkins
summary: The 2026 MLB season has officially begun, and with it comes the inaugural edition of Field Access, a collection of one-on-one conversations with both players and coaches with the Toronto Blue Jays.   To start the year, Blue Jays radio play-by-play voice Ben Shulman chats with Andrés Giménez (1:30), Braydon Fisher (4:35), and David Popkins (7:26).   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Mad Max Shuts Down Rockies, Jays Even Series
summary: Jeff Blair and Kevin Barker break down the Blue Jays' 5-1 win over the Rockies down at the Rogers Centre! They take your calls and texts, and get right to six innings of one-run ball from Max Scherzer - how needed is this with the rotation depth getting tested early in the season? They discuss Tyler Heineman both behind the plate and calling the game for Scherzer, the offence breaking through in the seventh, and also get to the usage of the bullpen. Plus, who is the long man for the Jays right now?   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Jays Get Rocked, Lose Ponce to Injury
summary: Jeff Blair and Kevin Barker break down the Blue Jays' 14-5 loss vs. the Rockies in the series opener at the Rogers Centre. They take your calls and texts, and get right to Cody Ponce departing the game early with an injury. Can the rotation weather another big injury if Ponce is forced to miss time? They discuss the struggles of Brendon Little - with one more minor league option remaining, is it time to send him down? Who could the team replace him with? Plus, Kazuma Okamoto rocked another home run - how has his play looked both offensively and defensively?   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: The Lauer Power Hour Returns, Jays Sweep A's
summary: Sho Alli and Jesse Rubinoff break down the Blue Jays' 5-2 win over the Athletics to open the season with a sweep at the Rogers Centre! They take your calls and texts, and get right to Eric Lauer's nine strikeouts over 5.1 IP - if the rotation is truly a meritocracy, could he force John Schneider's hand if he continues his strong start? They discuss the relief pitchers, including 1.2 IP from Braydon Fisher, and a save from Jeff Hoffman - should there be less angst about the bullpen? Plus, Jesús Sánchez and Kazuma Okamoto both clobbered their first Blue Jay home runs today, while George Springer added a solo shot as well - what could the rest of the season hold for Sánchez and Okamoto with their new teams?   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Clement Plays the Hero in Extras, Jays Nab (Another) Walk-off Win vs. A's
summary: Sho Alli and Jesse Rubinoff break down the Blue Jays' 8-7 walk-off win over the Athletics down at the Rogers Centre! They take your calls and texts, and get to Dylan Cease notching 12 strikeouts over 5.1 IP - what's the ceiling for Cease as the season continues? They discuss the bullpen, including some bad luck for Mason Fluharty, an uneven outing for Brendon Little, and on the flipside, an electric debut for Spencer Miles. Plus, they get into the offence, including the decision to intentionally walk Daulton Varsho to bring up Ernie Clement in extra innings, Alejandro Kirk blasting a game-tying home run in the bottom of the ninth, and Andrés Giménez adding four hits as he stays hot to start the year.   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: The Wizard of Gaus Fans 11, Jays Walk Off A's in Season Opener
summary: Jeff Blair and Kevin Barker break down the Blue Jays' 3-2 walk-off win over the Athletics to open the 2026 MLB season with a win! They take your calls and texts, and get right to Andrés Giménez delivering all three runs, including the game-winning RBI single - what could his production look like this season? They discuss Kevin Gausman fanning 11 batters over six innings of work - should he have stayed in for a seventh inning, or was it no sweat considering the early nature of the season? Plus, they discuss Jeff Hoffman giving up a home run in the ninth inning, and get into a good start for Ernie Clement.   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: JTP: Opening Day Special with Ben Nicholson-Smith & Jon Morosi!
summary: Blake Murphy is joined by MLB Insider & Sportsnet’s Ben Nicholson-Smith (2:24) to talk Dylan Cease’s potential, Kevin Gausman, Addison Barger’s upcoming season and much more. Afterwards, Jon Morosi comes in-studio (23:22) to talk Blue Jays, his World Baseball Classic experience, and the MLB season. The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Opening Day Roster is Set + 2026 Bold Predictions
summary: With the 2026 Blue Jays season right around the corner, Sho Alli and Daniele Franceschi convene for one last off-season edition of Jays Talk! They discuss the feeling around the team as the season begins - with all attention on the Jays (and not the Leafs or Raptors, at least in Toronto), is there added pressure? Sho gets into the 50th season installations down at the Rogers Centre ballpark tour (9:06), and they discuss the news that the final bullpen spot has gone to Spencer Miles, while Leo Jiménez has been DFA'd (16:48) to finalize the 26-man active roster for Opening Day. Plus, they break down the team via some 2026 bold predictions (31:36), and also hear the predictions of Blake Murphy (57:47), Ben Shulman (1:04:24), and Ben Nicholson-Smith (1:09:46).   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Is it Too Early to Be CONCERNED Over Sean Manaea's Velocity?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets fans are buzzing about Sean Manaea’s drop in velocity during spring training—could this spell trouble for the left-hander?   With his fastball averaging just 88.8 mph, will Manaea find a way to bounce back or risk losing his spot in the starting rotation? Ryan Finkelstein breaks down Manaea’s recent outings, examining pitch command, the effectiveness of his secondary offerings, and the impact of the Mets’ pitching depth.   Key discussion points include Carlos Mendoza’s comments on Manaea’s health. Can Manaea regain his form and solidify his role, or is New York poised for a shake-up?   This episode offers a thorough look at the Mets’ strategy as Opening Day approaches. title: Luis Robert Jr. Makes Spring Debut | Is Health Really the ONLY Thing Standing in Robert's Way?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets set high expectations for Luis Robert Jr. after his spring debut—can his health alone unlock All-Star potential, or do swing mechanics and motivation play a bigger role in his resurgence?   Host Ryan Finkelstein challenges the common narrative, spotlighting Robert’s swing changes, plate discipline issues, and the impact of a fresh environment on his performance. Key topics include Luis Robert Jr.'s injury history, defensive value in center field, and potential to mash left-handed pitching.   Ryan projects Robert’s likely stats, discusses the wide range of outcomes, and introduces the annual jersey bet for Mets fans.   Will Luis Robert Jr. rise to the challenge and become the star Mets fans crave?Everydayer Club    If you never miss an episode, it’s time to make it official. title: Meet some new Jays, plus Team Canada vs. Team U.S.A at the WBC
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Blue Jays relievers Tyler Rogers and Chase Lee, shortstop prospect Arjun Nimmala  This week in Deep Left Field, we dive into Canada's Pool A victory at the World Baseball Classic, the first time Team Canada has ever advanced beyond the first round of the WBC. Their reward is a quarter-final win-or-go-home match-up against a humiliated Team USA on Friday the 13th.  Then we introduce you to a couple of new Blue Jays:  Taylor Rogers, the submarining right-hander signed to a $37 million (US) contract in December, has been one of the best set-up men in the game for the past five years. Now his home ballpark has his name on all the trash cans.  Chase Lee was acquired in a much more below-the-radar transaction, a December trade with the Detroit Tigers, but he's someone you'll want to get to know. A sidearm righty who didn't make his college baseball team as a freshman but had a 1.098 WHIP as a rookie in the big leagues and also has a degree in Aerospace Engineering and Mechanics.  Also, meet Arjun Nimmala, one of the top-100 prospects in all of baseball and the Jays' first round pick in 2023!  All that, plus the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

title: Scott Van Pelt On The Baltimore Accent, The Masters and Classic Sportscenter Commercials
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: Back in 2024, we had the pleasure of welcoming our friend Scott Van Pelt to the podcast. For years, he and Tim Kurkjian have been staples on ESPN, but it’s their segments together that have truly made the internet smile. This episode originally ran on July 9th, 2024.   Their shared home state of Maryland gave rise to one of the great recurring bits — Scott cracking Tim up with his take on the Baltimore accent. If you’re a sports fan, you’ve probably seen one of these moments somewhere, whether on ESPN or circulating around the internet.  During our conversation, we talked about The Masters and Scott’s love of all things golf, as well as his upbringing as an Orioles fan and how that helped spark his passion for baseball — and plenty more along the way. We’re bringing this episode back as a thank-you to our new followers and subscribers.  We’ve got a lot of fun and exciting interviews coming your way in the 2026 season, and we’re thrilled to have you along for the journey. title: EXTENSION: Jesus Luzardo Lays the Blueprint for Freddy Peralta | How Much Will the Mets Give Him?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Philadelphia Phillies set a new market standard by locking in Jesús Luzardo on a 5-year, $135 million extension—have the New York Mets found the perfect blueprint for Freddy Peralta’s future?   Ryan Finkelstein unpacks how this deal resets the bar for elite southpaw starters, breaking down contract structures, luxury tax implications, and why leveraging arbitration years gives teams the upper hand. Key topics include comparisons to Ranger Suárez and Dylan Cease, detailed projections for a potential 7-year, $170 million Mets extension for Peralta, and creative contract elements like club options, signing bonuses, and trade kickers.   Finkelstein debates whether the Mets should strike before Opening Day or risk losing Peralta to a thinner free-agent market.   Can New York’s front office outmaneuver rivals and secure one of baseball’s most intriguing arms for the long term? title: World Baseball Classic Career Highlight, Pete Rose Loves Winning, Could Robinsons Beat Johnsons?
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: Tim Kurkjian and his son Jeff are back at it again—and this time the hype is all about the incredible World Baseball Classic and one player who says the experience ranks as a career highlight.  With the clocks set back, it means we’re one step closer to the start of the regular season. Tim also shares a few standout young players he had the chance to see during his time in Florida and Arizona.  Then there’s the matter of a Ron Howard appearance. After a recent text exchange with Buck Showalter, it suddenly doesn’t seem so impossible.  Pete Rose also comes up—specifically the stat he might be most proud of. In fact, he was so proud of it that he offered it to Tim without even being asked.  Plus, we rip open some baseball cards and uncover a minor-league moment from Larry Anderson that will absolutely make you smile.  Let’s load the cannon on another great episode. title: We go all Canadian from sunny Florida as the Jays continue spring training, plus we open the mailbag
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Blue Jays first baseman Vladimir Guerrero Jr., Team Canada pitcher Michael Soroka, Canadian Baseball Hall of Famer Russell Martin   This week, Deep Left Field comes to you from spring training once again and we present an all-Canadian episode!   Vladimir Guerrero Jr. (Montreal, Que.) joins us for his first-ever Deep Left Field conversation without an interpreter. We talk to Vladdy about the Jays' magical playoff run last season and what he thinks of his team going into 2026 as his half-billion dollar (US) contract kicks in.   With Team Canada having played a pair of games to prepare for the World Baseball Classic, which begins (for them) Saturday morning in Puerto Rico, we get the inside scoop on their attempt to finally break through and make it out of the first round of the WBC.   Michael Soroka (Calgary, AB) will start that first game against Colombia. He and former Blue Jay Russell Martin (East York, Ont.), who will be coaching first base for Canada, fill us in on everything you need to know about the red and white!   Plus, we open up the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!   Listen here or subscribe at Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or wherever you listen to your favourite podcasts. If you would like to support the journalism of the Toronto Star, you can at thestar.com/subscribe.

summary: Big partnership news to start the show! A huge thank you to  @foulterritoryshow  Network for bringing us on board for the 2026 season. One quick note: beginning in the 2026 season, we’ll be adjusting our episode schedule to better serve our audience. title: Jeff Hoffman reflects on Game 7, 'I had a chance to end it'
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guest: Blue Jays closer Jeff Hoffman       In this special bonus episode of Deep Left Field, from Blue Jays' spring training in Dunedin, Florida, we look back at the ninth inning of Game 7 of the World Series with the man who was on the mound for the Jays and gave up the first-ever ninth-inning game-tying home run in World Series history.   Jeff Hoffman said that day that he'd cost his teammates a World Series ring, and he reflects on that moment in an open, vulnerable and candid conversation - the first such interview he's done since last year's crushing loss.   We open up the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca as well, for your comments and questions!

title: Max is back! Myles Straw talks Scherzer, then we speak to three *imenezes - Andres G., Leo J. and Eloy J.!
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Blue Jays outfielder Myles Straw, shortstop Andres Gimenez, infielder Leo Jimenez, outfielder Eloy Jimenez  Deep Left Field returns to Dunedin, Florida for Blue Jays spring training and this week we present a confluence of *imenezes, each of whom has been making news in the early days of camp, but first, a chat with Myles Straw about the Jays repatriation of Max Scherzer, who agreed to a one-year deal early Thursday morning.  Andres Gimenez will be the Jays' starting shortstop this season. It's a move back to his original position, and one he played very well in the absence of Bo Bichette down the stretch and into the playoffs. Gimenez talks to us about shifting back to the other side of second base and what he needs to do to improve his offence.  Leo Jimenez is out of options, and in a make-or-break position to break camp as the back-up infielder or be put through waivers after a lost 2025 because of illness and injury. We catch up with the youngster, who was solid for the Jays in 2024, to talk about how he's dealing with the pressure.  And we play "meet the new guy" with Eloy Jimenez, a one-time top prospect who exploded onto the big-league scene with 47 home runs over his first 173 career games before running into the injury bug - hard - for five years. Here on a minor-league contract, he's off to a sensational start to the spring.  All this plus the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets

title: A Mazterclass
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: We said goodbye to an absolute legend, Bill Mazeroski. He also earned a spot on Tim’s All-Defense Team—alongside several other incredibly skilled defenders. We also recapped Jeff’s weekend adventure involving a pool of ice water (a bold but smart move, Jeff).  Eddie Murray was a major topic this week as we celebrated his birthday, along with so many other great baseball birthdays. We also remembered Tony C., lost on this day—perhaps the bravest of them all.  Dave Kingman’s baseball card made an appearance during the show, and honestly, he could probably still play in 2026—though maybe not for the best reasons. Tim shared some special memories from his very first Spring Training, especially fitting as he heads to much warmer weather this week (which we’re all looking forward to).  And next week, we’ve got absolutely MASSIVE news we can’t wait to share. If you’ve been following along, nothing will change for you—and the same goes for anyone new. But be sure to tune in next week for all the details!  Visit GreatGameOrWhat.com to contact the show with your questions, quips and insights.    Joy Pop Productions LLC See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: With spring training in full swing, we discuss how Kazuma Okamoto is looking, plus the mailbag
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Former Blue Jays catcher and broadcaster Alan Ashby; Toronto Star baseball columnist Gregor Chisholm  This week in Deep Left Field, we head down to Dunedin, Florida as the Star's Gregor Chisholm wraps up his week at Blue Jays spring training. We check in with Gregor about what he's seen from the Jays and get his thoughts on, among other things, how Kazuma Okamoto has been looking, why Anthony Santander didn't have his shoulder surgery much earlier and how Eric Lauer feels about being overlooked for a spot in the Jays' rotation once again.  Also, we get into the wayback machine as the Jays get set for their 50th anniversary season and talk to Alan Ashby, who caught 124 games in the Blue Jays inaugural season, 1977.  All that plus a dip into the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

title: INJURY NEWS: Brett Baty Limited to 1B, Nursing Hamstring Injury | Could This Derail His Move to RF?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets face crucial spring training questions as Brett Baty’s hamstring injury disrupts early plans for right field.   Will Carson Benge seize the opportunity and emerge as the Opening Day starter, or could veterans like Mike Tauchman and MJ Melendez force their way into the mix?   Prospects, position battles, and the lingering impact of injuries set the stage for a compelling preseason shakeup. Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the Mets’ cautious approach, touching on Luis Robert Jr., Francisco Álvarez, and Jorge Polanco’s slower progressions as the team prioritizes durability.   Insights include the potential squeeze on Mark Vientos at first base, how the Mets could leverage the Prospect Promotion Incentive if Benge wins Rookie of the Year, and why the club’s outfield configuration might be more fluid than anticipated.   With Opening Day looming, can the Mets optimize their roster and avoid setbacks from nagging injuries? title: WILD CARDS: Who's the BETTER Bounce Back Candidate: Sean Manaea or Kodai Senga?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Kodai Senga and Sean Manaea shift from rotation anchors to wild cards for the New York Mets—can either reclaim ace status in 2026?   Host Ryan Finkelstein breaks down why Senga’s season unraveled after a hamstring injury, spotlighting his soaring home run rate post-injury and the challenge of recapturing elite form.   Manaea’s own struggles are dissected, revealing how lost confidence and mental lapses sabotaged a season where his underlying metrics still flashed ace potential. With insights from new pitching coach Justin Willard and a focus on advanced stats like xFIP and Sierra, the discussion highlights the adjustments both pitchers are making—including Manaea’s return to a refined arm slot and revived changeup.   Projections, health concerns, and the Mets’ revamped pitching depth take center stage as Ryan Finkelstein weighs who is the better bounce-back candidate—and what it means for the team’s playoff hopes. podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: The New York Mets bet big on Bo Bichette as he arrived early at spring training, shifting from shortstop to third base. Can his glove hold up, and his bat make him one of MLB’s best at the hot corner?   Ryan Finkelstein explores Bichette’s defensive transition, examines coaching feedback, and argues how timing and range might shape his performance.   With Francisco Lindor and Marcus Semien anchoring the middle, Bichette’s arm strength and positioning spark debate: Will he surprise critics and outperform expectations defensively? Bichette’s offensive star power shines, potentially elevating the Mets' lineup alongside Juan Soto. Ryan Finkelstein compares Bichette statistically to Jose Ramirez, Alex Bregman, and Junior Caminero, highlighting his WRC+, clutch hitting, and rare ability to combine batting average and power.   Discover how Bichette ranks among MLB’s top third basemen heading into 2026, and whether he can climb past veteran rivals or youthful challengers. title: Grading EACH Move of the New York Mets Offseason | How Much Better Did They Get for 2026?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: The New York Mets overhauled their roster, sparking debates about their path to contention. Brandon Nimmo was traded, Pete Alonso walks in free agency, and major signings—including Devin Williams, Jorge Polanco, and Bo Bichette—reshape the team.   Did these bold moves improve defense and get the Mets closer to an NL East crown, or did letting Edwin Diaz and Tyler Rogers slip away leave the bullpen exposed? Host Ryan Finkelstein grades every transaction, examines the gains of Luis Robert and ace Freddy Peralta, and weighs the risks in the revamped lineup.   With concerns over left field depth and a bullpen missing its signature punch, can the Mets’ new strategy deliver an elite season, or will the costly roster reboot fall short?   Tune in for sharp analysis of the Mets’ offseason grades, long-term flexibility, and what fans can expect as spring training begins. title: Season Starts In 6-7 Weeks
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: The Super Bowl is DONE, which means for some teams, pitchers and catchers report TODAY! This is what we’ve been waiting for all offseason. Spring Training is here, and this is when our show hits primetime. We can’t thank you enough for being along for the ride — and don’t forget to hit subscribe! Today, we dive into Tim Kurkjian’s all-time favorite Spring Training stories, featuring warthogs, bicycles, and softball gloves. We also celebrate a few birthdays, including one from Australia (not something you see every day). We rip some cards and see what we can uncover — always a good time with “Three Cards in the Bathroom.” Tim may be a historian of the game, but there’s one never-achieved matchup he’ll defend forever. Soak it all in, folks. Spring Training has arrived, and Tim and Jeff Kurkjian are celebrating right along with you. Thanks for listening, and as always, thanks for being part of our family. title: Celebrating Buck Martinez ft. Hazel Mae + Jeff Blair
summary: After a legendary career as a broadcaster, manager, and player, Sportsnet's Buck Martinez has officially announced his retirement. To celebrate and look back on a phenomenal career, Sho Alli and Zack Bodenstein were joined on Sportsnet Today by both Hazel Mae (1:11) and Jeff Blair (10:51). They share their memories of Buck both on and off the field, why he is so beloved by fans around the country, and what made him a presence in our living rooms for decades.   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: DEBUT: Which Mets' Top Prospects Will Get to the SHOW in 2026? | Benge, Ross, Clifford, and More!
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets prospects are set to ignite the MLB stage—how many will rise to the challenge in 2026?   Carson Benge and Dylan Ross look primed for Opening Day debuts, while Ryan Clifford, AJ Ewing, Jacob Reimer, and Jack Weniger are poised as intriguing breakout candidates.   Is this the year the Mets’ farm system delivers its next wave of impact players? Ryan Finkelstein spotlights the Mets’ evolving roster, focusing on last year’s top prospect debuts, bullpen competitions, and future debuts.   The episode covers standout minor league performances, future roles for Nick Morabito, and bullpen flamethrowers battling command issues.   Fans get an inside look at how depth arms like Jonathan Pintaro and Alex Carillo could shape the team, and whether Mets rookies can surpass last year’s record nine debuts.   Will rising talent push the Mets to playoff contention or face roadblocks adjusting to Triple-A? title: Listen: The Class of 2026 Canadian Baseball Hall of Fame
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Canadian Baseball Hall of Fame Class of 2026 Inductees Stubby Clapp, Kate Psota, Paul Runge, Bill Stoneman, Devon White  This week in Deep Left Field, we speak to every living member of the just-announced Class of 2026 for the Canadian Baseball Hall of Fame. They will be inducted in a ceremony in St. Marys, Ont. on June 20.  Devon White, the greatest centrefielder in Blue Jays' history.  Stubby Clapp, perhaps the living embodiment of the Canadian national team player.  Kate Psota, one of the first superstar women's players in modern history.  Bill Stoneman, who threw the first of his two no-hitters for the Montreal Expos in the ninth game in franchise history.  Paul Runge, a St. Catharines, Ont. native who spent a quarter-century as a major-league umpire, including working the 1993 World Series between the Blue Jays and Philadelphia Phillies.  All those immortals of Canadian baseball, plus your thoughts in the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

title: If the Mets BELIEVE in Carson Benge, EXTEND Him! | The Case for a Pre-Arb Extension to Start 2026
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets set the stage for a big 2026 with Carson Benge as their potential everyday left fielder—will the team lock up their prized prospect with a pre-arbitration extension?   Excitement grows as David Stearns expresses unwavering confidence in Benge, prompting questions about service time manipulation, PPI eligibility, and the blueprint for early-career deals that could shape the future of the franchise. Ryan Finkelstein breaks down recent trends in MLB contract extensions, examining deals like Luis Robert’s six-year, $50 million contract, Jackson Chourio’s record-setting pre-debut pact, and the unique incentives around the prospect promotion incentive (PPI).   Key topics include the strategic impact of Benge’s service time decisions, historical extension precedents, and the Mets’ outfield roster with stars like Juan Soto and Luis Robert.   Can the Mets maximize both Benj’s talent and their draft capital as Opening Day approaches? title: He Pulled A Grammy
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: Tim and Jeff Kurkjian can’t help but be fascinated (once again) by the lingering snow from the storm—though their dogs are clearly over it. Big shoutout to our SABR friends in Las Vegas: thanks for having us, and welcome to the family to those who are new! Jeff quizzes Tim on Grammy nominees and winners, and surprisingly, Tim holds his own. How would you have done? If you love this country, you’ll love Team Tim, as we take a tour through America’s cities. We also dive into two major free-agent signings, with Tim breaking down the impact of Eugenio Suárez and Luis Arraez. And don’t even mention Pinewood Derby to Tim—or karate to Jeff—trust us, both are sore subjects. We also reminisce about some great players, including the “tenth man,” Will Clark (you’ll have to tune in to find out what that means). We’re hoping to grow our YouTube following, and it’s easy to help: search “Is This A Great Game, Or What?” on YouTube and hit subscribe. You can watch the full show in video form—how neat is that? Sure, we’re easy on the eyes, but the reactions and laughs are even better on video. Thanks so much for supporting the show! title: WBC insurance controversy, Puerto Rico's stance + Richie Shaffer joins FT to discuss mental game
summary: WBC Reporter and Content Creator Shawn Spradling joins FT to break down the growing insurance issues surrounding the World Baseball Classic and why Puerto Rico has been severely impacted. He explains how insurance decisions are made, who carries the risk if a player gets hurt, and what it could mean for the tournament if teams are unable to secure coverage for their stars.  (15:36) Former Rays first-round pick Richie Shaffer joins FT to discuss how players prepare for camp at different stages of their careers and why mental skills are often overlooked in player development. Shaffer also reflects on his experience in Tampa Bay’s development system, why teams still underinvest in preparing players mentally, the risks of overhyping prospects, and why he remains cautiously optimistic the Rays ultimately stay in Tampa. podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Eugenio Suarez signs a shockingly low one-year, $15 million deal with the Cincinnati Reds, raising questions about the New York Mets’ decision to lock in Jorge Polanco for two years at $40 million.   Did the Mets jump the gun in free agency, and will the move pay off as they seek to reinvent their lineup after losing Pete Alonso? Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the Mets’ offseason strategy, comparing Suarez, Polanco, and Ryan O’Hearn’s contracts, durability, and projected value.   The conversation highlights Polanco’s fit at first base, risks around his consistency and health, and whether front office urgency led to an overpay.   Key topics include lineup construction, defensive upgrades, and the ripple effects on Mark Vientos and Brett Baty’s roles.   Mets fans get the data and analysis on whether Polanco can match Alonso’s impact—or if the team missed out on wiser roster options. title: Another INSIDE Look at How the 2026 Mets Came Together | Where Did the Mets MISS This Winter?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets orchestrate a dramatic offseason overhaul, parting ways with Pete Alonso, Jeff McNeil, and Brandon Nimmo to reshape their defensive core.   Can Bo Bichette, Marcus Semien, and Luis Robert deliver the spark Mets fans crave?   Ryan Finkelstein breaks down inside info from The Athletic, detailing the front office’s aggressive pursuit of defensive upgrades and high-upside plays that transformed the roster for 2026. Explore the Mets’ calculated decisions to bypass Alonso’s return, weigh costly bullpen moves like the Edwin Díaz and Devin Williams saga, and analyze the potential payoffs—and risks—of betting big on newcomers.   Key topics include the failed Kyle Tucker chase, alternative trade targets like Stephen Kwan and Lars Nootbaar, and concerns about bullpen depth.   Will these bold moves finally get the Mets back into contention, or did they miss a crucial opportunity with Díaz?   Don’t miss this essential analysis of Queens’ high-stakes offseason. title: Will the Mets REGRET Trading Jett Williams? | Jett vs. A.J. Ewing, Freddy Peralta Trade Analysis
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Did the New York Mets pay too steep a price for Freddy Peralta—or will moving top prospect Jett Williams haunt their future?   Host Ryan Finkelstein and prospect expert Aram Leighton break down the blockbuster Mets-Brewers trade, weighing the value of arms like Brandon Sproat against the loss of dynamic talents such as Williams.   The show tackles whether Peralta can be New York’s missing ace and how Tobias Myers factors into the big league roster, while questioning if David Stearns made the right call pushing prospects to win now. The discussion heats up with a head-to-head comparison of Jett Williams and AJ Ewing, diving into advanced metrics, positional value, and each player’s ceiling for the Mets’ future.   Insights on Jacob Reimer, Ryan Clifford, Jack Wenninger, and Carson Benge give listeners a full rundown of the team’s farm system depth.   Plus, rapid-fire predictions on Francisco Alvarez, Brett Baty, and Mark Vientos highlight how the Mets’ young core will shape the upcoming season. title: Baseball's CBA is expiring this year, is another lockout looming? Plus, the mailbag
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Toronto Star baseball columnist Gregor Chisholm, former New York Post baseball columnist, now journalism professor and author Ken Davidoff  This week in Deep Left Field, we talk labour. Baseball's Collective Bargaining Agreement expires this year and the stage is set for another off-season lockout, just like we saw five years ago. There's no question that the 2026 season will be played, but questions about 2027 loom large and we have convened a Deep Left Field panel to try to answer them.  What do the owners want? What about the players? How united is each side? Will there be a work stoppage - the first since the 1994 strike - that delays the start of the 2027 season or worse?  My Blue Jays beat partner Gregor Chisholm and longtime Yankees' columnist Ken Davidoff, author of the new book "101 Lessons From The Dugout" join me and we try to figure things out.  All this, plus your thoughts and questions in the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

title: Maury Brown’s report on Dodgers revenue-sharing shelter & CBA battles
summary: The Dodgers are in the spotlight once again, and we brought in Forbes Senior Contributor Maury Brown to clear things up. He breaks down why an old, court-set number from the Dodgers’ bankruptcy era is stealing headlines, how it affects what gets counted toward revenue sharing, the difference between projected “fair market value” and what the Dodgers’ media rights ultimately became, and what that means for the broader debate about competitive balance, transparency, and the next CBA. title: PERFECT FIT: Freddy Peralta Should Be a Building Block, NOT a Rental!
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Will the Mets seize the opportunity to make Freddy Peralta the rotation’s foundation?   Host Ryan Finkelstein examines Peralta’s powerful press conference and follow-up interview, revealing why the former Milwaukee Brewers ace and lifelong Mets fan could be the perfect fit for New York.   Discussion centers on Peralta’s talent, charisma, and leadership, plus his deep ties to the city and frontrunners like David Stearns. Key topics include potential contract extension scenarios—could a six-year, $140 million deal secure Peralta’s future as the Mets’ ace?   The episode breaks down payroll implications, the value of building around Peralta, and how locking him in sends a strong message to the fanbase.   With big decisions looming, Ryan Finkelstein asks: Will the Mets commit now and craft a championship-caliber rotation, or risk losing one of baseball’s most promising arms? title: Are the ONCE 'Baby Mets' Part of the New CORE? | Baty, Alvarez, and Vientos Set For a PIVOTAL Year
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets enter a defining season as Mark Vientos, Brett Baty, and Francisco Alvarez get their shot to become the next Mets core.   With Pete Alonso, Brandon Nimmo, and Jeff McNeil gone, host Ryan Finkelstein examines whether these former top prospects can stabilize the lineup or if 2026 marks their last ride in Queens.   Are Vientos’ power bat, Baty's rising versatility, and Alvarez’s defensive prowess enough to anchor the new era behind stars Francisco Lindor and Juan Soto? Key talking points include ZiPS projections for Vientos, Baty, and Alvarez, the power void left by recent trades, and the challenge of converting prospect promise into consistent big league production.   Finkelstein explores lineup possibilities, long-term team control, and which player could emerge as an everyday fixture for the Mets.   With new faces and high expectations, can these young hitters help shape a legitimate contender at Citi Field? They get into his leadoff home run to kickstart Game 5 of the World Series, how team chemistry was so special this past year, the departure of Bo Bichette, what it's like to play for John Schneider, and just what exactly 'nasty soup' is and how it came to be.   Kristina Rutherford's Davis Schneider Piece for Sportsnet.ca: https://www.sportsnet.ca/mlb/article/why-regular-dude-davis-schneider-will-take-on-any-job-to-help-the-blue-jays-win/   The views and opinions expressed in this podcast are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the position of Rogers Sports & Media or any affiliates.

title: Who's the BETTER Bounce Back Candidate: Marcus Semien or Luis Robert Jr.?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Can Luis Robert Jr. reclaim his All-Star form, or will Marcus Semien power a New York Mets resurgence at age 35?   The Mets’ recent defensive upgrades spark debate: which new addition has a better shot at bouncing back offensively and transforming the lineup this season? Ryan Finkelstein spotlights Luis Robert Jr.’s elite bat speed, base-stealing potential, and revamped mechanics—questioning whether his move to Citi Field can reignite his superstar ceiling.   The episode breaks down Marcus Semien’s reliable glove, leadership, and changes in plate discipline, while scrutinizing his declining home run power and aged bat.   Key insights touch on past WAR (Wins Above Replacement), WRC+, and each player’s track record against lefties.   With a sharper focus on up-the-middle defense, the Mets’ calculated risks come into full view. title: Oh, Snow Way!
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: SNOWED IN! We had a blast recording this episode from home—both of us snowed in (outside, of course). Tim was rescued by the plows just in time to hit record. Jeff, meanwhile, somehow still made it to his full-time job. On This Date in Baseball History, we tip our caps to “my man” and Harold Reynolds. And whenever Tim breaks out the cards, you know it’s going to be a good time. We also settle the debate on the greatest player to wear No. 59—and yes, it’s a current player! Plus, a puzzling story from Tim involving his grandson proves that even a guy with a massive baseball brain can struggle when it comes to actual puzzles. All this and more on this week’s edition of Is This a Great Game, or What? title: Have the Mets Done Enough to IMPROVE Their Bullpen? | Other Moves That Could Still Be Made
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets sign Craig Kimbrel to a minor-league deal, sparking questions about the bullpen’s future. Is a blockbuster move still looming, or will Kimbrel help anchor the late innings?   Ryan Finkelstein analyzes recent roster shuffles, the departure of key arms like Edwin Díaz and Tyler Rogers, and the addition of standout relievers Devin Williams, Luke Weaver, and Luis Garcia. Insights center on the Mets’ current bullpen construction, lingering gaps, and challenges with free-agent depth as potential upgrades like Seranthony Dominguez sign elsewhere.   Finkelstein explores possible trade targets such as Adrian Morejon, Bryan Abreu, JoJo Romero, and Victor Vodnik, debating whether a swap involving David Peterson could provide that missing high-leverage weapon.   With Opening Day approaching, will the Mets front office roll with this group or add another late-inning difference-maker?   Find out which moves could shape their season and the factors that could make this bullpen elite. title: ACE Effect: Do the Mets Have the BEST Rotation in the NL East? | Peralta's Impact Trickles Down
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets emerge as NL East rotation favorites after landing Freddy Peralta.   Can this star-powered pitching staff outshine the Braves and Phillies in 2026? Ryan Finkelstein breaks down how Freddy Peralta’s arrival transforms the Mets’ rotation, lowering the pressure on arms like Kodai Senga and Sean Manaea and offering crucial depth with Noah McLean, David Peterson, and Clay Holmes.   The discussion covers injury risks for division rivals, the Mets’ impressive top-nine starting pitcher depth—featuring prospects Jonah Tong and Christian Scott—and why competitors like the Phillies and Braves face major question marks.   Key topics include roster strategy, potential last-minute moves, and whether the Mets should chase additional pitching or trust their current lineup heading into spring camp. Is this rotation built for a deep postseason run, or will unproven talent and rival aces shake things up?   Don’t miss Ryan Finkelstein’s expert analysis on the Mets’ blueprint for NL East dominance. podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets eye a crucial turning point as ace Freddy Peralta expresses openness to a contract extension.   Will David Stearns seize the opportunity and lock down Peralta as the centerpiece of the Mets’ rotation for years to come? Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the Mets’ potential investment in Peralta, spotlighting the former Brewers pitcher’s rise as a strikeout machine and clubhouse leader.   Key talking points include Peralta’s connection with Stearns, extension comparisons to stars like Dylan Cease and Ranger Suarez, and the impact on future free-agent options like Tarik Skubal or Shane Bieber.   The discussion also explores how adding Peralta long-term could shape the Mets’ financial flexibility, influence young arms like Nolan McLean and Christian Scott, and drive roster decisions heading into the 2026 season. Are the Mets ready to build their championship core around Peralta—or will they risk missing out on elite talent in next year’s loaded free agent class? summary: New York Mets fans get the answers they've been craving—David Stearns transforms the Mets into legitimate World Series contenders with bold roster moves and creative front office maneuvering.   Can this reinvented core outshine last year's team and finally dethrone NL East rivals like the Braves and Phillies? Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the Mets' dramatic offseason overhaul: moving on from Brandon Nimmo, Pete Alonso, and Jeff McNeil, acquiring impact names like Marcus Semien, Devin Williams, Jorge Polanco, Bo Bichette, Luis Robert, and ace Freddy Peralta, plus smart bullpen additions like Luis Garcia.   Key discussion points include defensive upgrades, locker room leadership, comparing lineups and rotations, and what, if anything, remains for Stearns before Opening Day.   With tough decisions ahead for Mark Vientos and Kodai Senga, will the new-look Mets live up to the hype as the division’s best and a World Series favorite? title: BREAKING: Mets Land an ACE, Trade for Freddy Peralta! | Part with Jett and Sproat in Blockbuster
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets make a franchise-defining splash, acquiring ace Freddy Peralta from the Milwaukee Brewers in a blockbuster trade that sends top prospects Jett Williams and Brandon Sproat packing.   Is this the move that cements the Mets as National League East favorites? Host Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the high-stakes swap, weighing the Mets’ willingness to part with two Top 100 prospects for a workhorse starter with a stellar 2.70 ERA.   The analysis unpacks how Peralta slots into a fortified rotation alongside Kodai Senga and Nolan McLean, the potential impact of team-controlled arm Tobias Myers, and the shifting outlook for young talents like AJ Ewing.   Other hot topics include improvements in run prevention with defensive additions, bullpen upgrades after landing Luis Garcia, and whether the Mets’ aggressive offseason activity makes them the best team in the National League.   Are Steve Cohen and David Stearns building a serious World Series contender? title: STEAL: Mets Trade for Luis Robert Jr.! | Only Have to Deal Acuña and Low-A Pitching Prospect
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets land Luis Robert Jr. in a blockbuster trade with the Chicago White Sox, solidifying their outfield and avoiding a major prospect haul. Could this move elevate the Mets into true contenders, or will questions linger around Robert’s consistency and health? Ryan Finkelstein reacts to the surprise deal, breaking down why sending Luisangel Acuña and 12th-round pick Truman Pauley to Chicago is a win for the Mets’ lineup flexibility and defensive strength.   Key topics include Luis Robert Jr.’s potential impact in center field, the shakeup’s effect on prospects like Carson Benge and Jett Williams, and how staying out of the Cody Bellinger chase benefits team construction.   Elijah Evans joins to provide an insider White Sox perspective, discussing the deal’s implications for Chicago’s long-term rebuild and the uncertain future for Acuña in a crowded infield. title: Front and Center(Field)
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: Carlos Beltrán and Andruw Jones will join Jeff Kent as the National Baseball Hall of Fame Class of 2026. Longtime Hall of Fame voter Tim Kurkjian weighs in with his thoughts, including the one other player he voted for who fell short this year. We break down Beltrán’s playoff performances, along with the lingering controversy surrounding sign-stealing. title: Dodgers Sign Tucker, Mets Sign Bo, and What It Means for the Jays
summary: Blake Murphy and Kevin Barker are in for an offseason edition to react to Kyle Tucker signing with the Los Angeles Dodgers, the New York Mets signing Bo Bichette, and what it means for the Blue Jays. They start with Bo and what the Jays' lineup will look like without their long-time shortstop and his fit at third base with the Mets. Then, they discuss where the Jays may turn for an impact bat, how aggressively they should approach Cody Bellinger's market, and whether adding to the bullpen is a higher priority at this point. Finally, Blake and Kevin look at Cardinals' infielder Brendan Donovan as a potential trade target, and where the Jays stand in an improved AL East if their offseason work is now complete. title: The face of the "Glue Jays," Ernie Clement, plus Blue Jays GM Ross Atkins
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Blue Jays infielder Ernie Clement, general manager Ross Atkins   The face of the "Glue Jays," Ernie Clement, moves from the infield out to Deep Left Field to join us on the show this week. He talks about the immediate aftermath of Game 7 of the World Series in the clubhouse and gives his thoughts on the Jays' magical season and his magical post-season that fell just short. As well, Ernie fills us in on the pitch he made to Bo Bichette to stay with the Jays during Bichette's wedding.  Also, the 2025 Blue Jays were given a Special Recognition Award at last weekend's Baseball Canada awards banquet. Ross Atkins joined me on stage to accept the award and for an interview, which you can hear here.  Plus, as always, we open the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

title: Don Mattingly A.K.A. Donnie Baseball (And Sons)
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: Don Mattingly just took a job that lets him work alongside his son, so we couldn’t think of a better guest to have on. Oh—and he also happens to be one of Tim’s favorite players to cover back in the day.  We cover everything from fishing for 200-pound fish to talking hitting with Ted Williams, plus a shared love of country music and Don’s questionable knowledge of Lou Gehrig. Widely known as one of the best guys in the game, Mattingly has done it all. From managing the Marlins and Dodgers to serving as bench coach for the Blue Jays—and now the Phillies—whether he’s playing, coaching, or managing, he’s always got stories to tell.  We loved bringing you this exclusive offseason interview. title: What Do the Mets NEED Most Before Spring Training? | Evaluating Roster Need Post Core-Teardown
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets overhaul their lineup, but will these bold moves spark real improvement—or leave the team searching for answers?   With Pete Alonso, Brandon Nimmo, and Jeff McNeil out and Marcus Semien, Jorge Polanco, and Carson Benge in, Ryan Finkelstein unpacks what the dramatic roster shakeup means for run production and outfield defense.   Can the Mets’ new core, featuring top prospects and free agent targets like Cody Bellinger, fill the massive void left by established stars? Ryan Finkelstein assesses the bullpen’s makeover with Devin Williams replacing Edwin Diaz, and explores rotation options from Framber Valdez or Chris Bassitt to internal breakout candidates Nolan McLean and Brandon Sproat.   Key questions include whether a revamped run prevention strategy offsets the offensive losses, and if management’s new direction puts the Mets closer to playoff contention or facing another year in limbo.   Can this retooled squad succeed, or are more moves needed before Opening Day? title: Effectively Wild Episode 2419: TJ and TK in Triple-A
podcast_name: Effectively Wild: A FanGraphs Baseball Podcast
summary: Ben Lindbergh talks to two pitchers who topped out at Triple-A in 2025, which was a demotion for one and a promotion for the other. First, he brings back Rangers pitcher Declan Cronin, the only major leaguer alum of Ben’s high school, to discuss a setback of a season that included a mysterious hip injury, compromised mechanics, diminished stuff, a cutthroat release and, finally, Tommy John surgery. Along the way, they cover the urge to play through injuries, joining the Rangers after being unceremoniously jettisoned by the Marlins, what tearing a UCL feels like, the incentives that lead to elbow problems, Declan’s plan to bounce back from a challenging year, MLB-NWSL romances, and competing with other players for wedding dates. Then (1:05:45), Ben talks to White Sox pitcher/sportswriter Duncan Davitt about becoming a journalist on the side, being on both ends of interviews, being blacked out of baseball broadcasts, the plight and importance of local media, his deceptive delivery, being traded and making a 40-man for the first time, what he has to do to earn a call-up, whether pitchers wear protective cups, and much more. title: Welcome Cody Ponce, the Jays newly signed pitcher
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: New Blue Jays pitcher Cody Ponce, former Blue Jays pitcher Jay Jackson      This week in Deep Left Field, we meet Cody Ponce, the right-hander the Jays signed earlier this month who is coming off the greatest season ever pitched in the Korea Baseball Organization.  Ponce was introduced to the Toronto media this week, and we have highlights from that conversation, including what drew him to the Jays, his relationship with Hyun-Jin Ryu, what's changed since he was drummed out of the big leagues in 2021 and his deep and abiding love for all things Star Wars.  Also, Jay Jackson joins us to give us an update on baby JR, who spent the first few months of his life in the NICU while his dad was pitching for the Blue Jays and is now a healthy, happy 2 1/2-year-old. Jackson also fills us in on new Jays acquisition Tyler Rogers, with whom he pitched for the San Francisco Giants in 2021, and gives some straight talk on how he believes the Jays have handled Bo Bichette's free agency so far.  Plus, we open the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca - keep those cards and letters coming!

title: You Can’t Tell Me the Mets Will Be BETTER Without Pete Alonso | How Will David Stearns Try?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets face a pivotal moment after letting slugger Pete Alonso walk in free agency.   Can the lineup recover without their iconic first baseman, or has the franchise made a costly mistake that fans won’t soon forget? Host Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the emotional fallout from Alonso’s departure, weighing the impact on fan loyalty and locker room chemistry.   The discussion explores potential replacements like Jorge Polanco, Japanese stars Kazuma Okamoto and Munetaka Murakami, and trade targets including Jarren Duran and Luis Robert Jr.   Key topics include the Mets’ evolving defensive strategy, front office philosophy under David Stearns, and the challenge of building a contender around Juan Soto and Francisco Lindor.   Is a World Series run still within reach, or will Mets fans be left longing for the power of the Polar Bear? title: Mets and Padres Are Talking BLOCKBUSTER Trades | Who Should They Be After, and What’s OFF the Table?
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets and San Diego Padres spark trade buzz as Mason Miller, Nick Pivetta, and Adrian Morejon emerge as pivotal pieces that could reshape rosters. Can the Mets land a flamethrower closer with four years of control, or will they pursue a more attainable setup man to fortify their bullpen?   Host Ryan Finkelstein breaks down how massive prospect capital, like Jonah Tong or Jet Williams, might come into play—raising the question: should the Mets prioritize pitching depth or a long-term solution in the outfield? title: The Jays introduce newly signed Dylan Cease, plus we hear from newly-elected Hall of Famer Jeff Kent and we open the mailbag
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guest: Toronto Star Baseball Columnist Gregor Chisholm  This week in Deep Left Field, we wrap up the winter meetings with my partner on the Star's Blue Jays beat, Gregor Chisholm, who was in Orlando to watch the Jays introduce free-agent signee Dylan Cease and to talk to general manager Ross Atkins about the Jays' winter plans, Shane Bieber's forearm fatigue and Jose Berrios' state of mind, among other things.  We also hear from newly-elected Hall of Famer Jeff Kent, baseball's all-time leader in home runs by a second baseman. Kent was drafted and developed by the Jays and, until he was traded for David Cone in August, was the only rookie on the eventual World Series champion 1992 Blue Jays for most of the season.  And, as always, we open up the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca to hear from you!

title: What’s the Plan B, Post-Alonso? | ‘Disciplined’ Mets Have A TON of Options on the Table
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: New York Mets’ disciplined free agency strategy shakes up the roster as fan favorites Pete Alonso and Brandon Nimmo depart, sparking debate over the team’s direction. Will sacrificing star power ultimately create a stronger contender for 2026? Ryan Finkelstein scrutinizes the Mets’ pivot from big-ticket spending under Steve Cohen to targeted moves designed for sustainability.  Key topics include Brandon Nimmo’s trade, Edwin Diaz’s exit, and the pursuit of value contracts—like landing Devin Williams to replace Diaz.  The analysis spotlights opportunities in the trade market, the appeal of adding Wilson Contreras or Cody Bellinger, and the challenge of assembling a championship-caliber infield around Francisco Lindor and Juan Soto.  Mets fans, can patience with David Stearns’ bold reset lead to a perennial playoff run, or will nostalgia for stars lost haunt the new regime? title: HEARTBREAK: Pete Alonso LEAVES Mets for Orioles—Fanbase RAGES & Stearns’ Strategy is Questioned
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: Pete Alonso signs with the Baltimore Orioles, leaving New York Mets fans reeling and the franchise at a crossroads.   Is David Stearns orchestrating a bold reset, or have the Mets lost the core that brought hope to Queens?   Ryan Finkelstein breaks down the emotional and strategic fallout of Alonso’s five-year, $155 million deal, the gut punch of losing Brandon Nimmo and Edwin Diaz, and a fanbase desperate for answers. With major roster voids and no clear Plan B at first base, the discussion centers on Mets’ off-season options—will stars like Cody Bellinger, or Kyle Tucker fill the gap, or is more upheaval coming?   Key topics include Stearns’ unpopular moves, Francisco Lindor’s leadership, and the unforgiving New York spotlight as the Mets ponder their next blockbuster.   Don’t miss this passionate analysis of the Mets’ rapidly shifting future and the questions that could define their upcoming season. title: Kent Stop The Feeling
podcast_name: Is This A Great Game, Or What?
summary: Tim and Jeff Kurkjian dive into the election of Jeff Kent to the National Baseball Hall of Fame—plus the players who just missed the cut, the steroid-era debates, and more. It’s a deeper dive than you usually hear from these two, but a warranted one, offering an interesting contrast between Jeff (born in 1993), who grew up watching these players, and Tim, who has been a Hall of Fame voter for what feels like 150 years. We also talk Christmas décor as both homes get fully decked out. Tim’s tree is so massive he couldn’t set it up on his own—honestly admirable that he asked for help, because most of us men wouldn’t. And Tim’s outfit this episode, while improved, is still far too nice for our usual format. Watch on YouTube to see it for yourself, and don’t forget to subscribe! We wrap up with a look at notable moments in baseball history that happened on this date, and Tim closes the show with an excellent Team Tim, fittingly all about birthdays—since tomorrow is his! Thanks for listening, and we wish you and your family health and happiness! Visit GreatGameOrWhat.com to contact the show with your questions, quips and insights.    Joy Pop Productions LLC  Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. title: Who is Cody Ponce, the newest Blue Jay? Plus, Gord Ash on what winter meetings are like for a GM
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guests: Gord Ash, VP Baseball Projects, Milwaukee Brewers; Jee-ho Yoo, Yonhap News Agency  This week in Deep Left Field, we get you set for Major League Baseball's annual swap meet and free-agent signing session, the winter meetings, by giving you a look from the inside. Canadian Baseball Hall of Famer Gord Ash joins us to talk about what the meetings are like from a general manager's perspective. Ash was the Blue Jays' GM from 1995-2001 after working under Pat Gillick for six seasons.  We also take you across the world to South Korea, where the Jays dove into the international market and pulled out right-hander Cody Ponce, who had a record-setting season with the Hanwha Eagles of the KBO, winning the pitching triple crown while establishing new league marks in ERA and strikeouts. Jee-ho Yoo, who spent his university days in Toronto, joins us from the far east to tell us what he saw from Ponce this season and what Jays fans can expect.  And, as always, we open up the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca

title: Blue Jays Get a Jump on Winter Shopping
summary: The Blue Jays have flipped the narrative, jumping the market to land one of free agency’s biggest names well in advance of the Winter Meetings. Ben and Arden start by discussing the upside potential and downside risk of Toronto’s deal with Dylan Cease (00:20). Then, they cast forward to how the Blue Jays can approach Bo Bichette, Kyle Tucker, and the rest of the position player market (36:16). Finally, after the Blue Jays agreed to terms with Cody Ponce moments following this episode's original recording and rendered its final segment unusable, Arden jumps back in for a solo conclusion, offering his thoughts on what we can glean from the deferral structure of Dylan Cease’s contract and why the club is betting on the KBO MVP’s breakout (51:38). title: Winter Is Coming

title: What We Learned From the 2025 Fantasy Baseball Season
podcast_name: Rates & Barrels: A show about Baseball
summary: Eno and DVR are back from First Pitch Arizona to discuss a few things they learned from the 2025 season. The decision to maintain a more balanced approach to pitching on draft day helped Eno in a big way last season as the 21-40 range Starting Pitchers continue to provide plenty of added value. They look at some of the inexperienced risers in that range for 2026 including Nolan McLean, Jacob Misiorowski, Cam Schlittler, Chase Burns, and Trey Yesavage. Plus, they wonder if spring injury news should carry a higher level of importance when making draft-day decisions, and they look ahead at a few early-round surprises by November average draft position (ADP). title: Don Mattingly's Blue Jays exit interview
podcast_name: Deep Left Field with Mike Wilner
summary: Guest: Former Blue Jays bench coach Don Mattingly  After three years with the Jays, Don Mattingly feels that his work is done and he is moving on. But before the 64-year-old tackles the next challenge in a life that has seen him involved in Major League Baseball since the New York Yankees drafted him in 1979, he joins us in Deep Left Field to talk about his time in Toronto.  Mattingly went to the World Series for the first time this season and he discusses what they could have done better in the late innings of Game 7, but also compares this Jays team to his old Yankees teams in the mid-90s that would go on to build a dynasty.  Also, as always, we open up the mailbag at deepleftfield@thestar.ca!

title: TRADE RUMORS: Byron Buxton MAY Get Traded After ALL! | Should the Mets Call the Twins This Winter?

summary: The New York Mets could transform their outfield and rotation if Byron Buxton hits the trade market. Is a blockbuster move brewing in Queens?   Ryan Finkelstein explores the possibility of acquiring Buxton, Joe Ryan, or Pablo Lopez from the Minnesota Twins, weighing the risks and rewards in a center field market thin on elite talent. Trade rumors swirl as Finkelstein assesses mock deals, prospect packages, and team needs—factoring in names like Mark Vientos, Brandon Sproat, and AJ Ewing.   The episode spotlights Scott Boras's latest statements on Pete Alonso, the Mets’ interest in Cody Bellinger, and how GM meetings could reshape roster decisions.   With the Mets targeting key upgrades and facing tough choices at first base and in the outfield, the stakes for their 2026 campaign rise.   Will New York pursue star power or trust homegrown talent like Carson Benge to turn the tide? title: Mets Trade RUMORS: Ketel Marte is ON the Block | Kodai Senga Drawing 'Buy-Low' Trade Interest
podcast_name: Locked On Mets - Daily Podcast On The New York Mets
summary: The New York Mets could be eyeing a blockbuster move as Ketel Marte hits the trade block, potentially slotting between Francisco Lindor and Juan Soto in a dream lineup.   The Arizona Diamondbacks' All-Star second baseman, who finished third in MVP voting in 20234, brings elite production at a discount—$92 million remaining through 2030 represents exceptional value for baseball's best at his position.   With Arizona motivated to move him before his full no-trade clause kicks in mid-April, the window for acquiring the switch-hitting superstar is narrow. Host Ryan Finkelstein breaks down what a competitive trade package might look like, discussing prospects like Brandon Sproat, Jett Williams, and Christian Scott. He examines how Marte's acquisition could reshape the Mets' roster construction, potentially affecting Pete Alonso's future in Queens.   The episode also covers Kodai Senga drawing trade interest, Jose Rojas signing a minor league deal, and Andy Martino's latest intel on the Alonso situation, challenging narratives about David Stearns' intentions. Discover how the Mets could transform their lineup with this potential game-changing acquisition and what it means for their championship aspirations.
